summary: cookie-parser-js fetches and eval()s remote code from a base64-obfuscated endpoint
details: cookie-parser-js impersonates the well-known cookie-parser middleware (matching author metadata, README, and repo field). When the exported cookieParser factory runs during Express middleware setup, the module issues an axios GET to a base64-obfuscated URL that decodes to https://cookie-api-two.vercel.app/ and passes the response body directly to eval(), giving the operator of that host arbitrary code execution inside the installer's Node.js process. The destination is hidden via atob() rather than a plain literal, and the fetched content is unpinned and unverified, so the attacker can change the delivered payload at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
